title: McIlroy's Back-to-Back: Northern Irishman Retains Green Jacket in Thrilling Masters Finale
excerpt: Rory McIlroy holds off Scottie Scheffler to win his second consecutive Masters at Augusta National, claiming his sixth major championship.
body:
McIlroy Seals Historic Repeat at Augusta

Rory McIlroy added his name to one of golf's most exclusive rolls of honour on Sunday, winning the 2026 Masters Tournament for the second consecutive year. The Northern Irishman finished at 12 under par (276), holding off world No. 1 Scottie Scheffler by a single stroke in what became a thrilling final-day duel at Augusta National.

McIlroy is only the fourth player in Masters history to win back-to-back Green Jackets, joining an elite company: Jack Nicklaus, Nick Faldo, and Tiger Woods. With this victory, his sixth major championship, the 36-year-old moves level with legends Nick Price and Lee Trevino on the all-time major wins list.

Winning Score
-12 (276)
Runner-Up
Scheffler -11
First Prize
$4.5m
Major Titles
6

A Week of Composure

What distinguished McIlroy's performance was his remarkable consistency. He held a share of the lead after every single round — a rarity at Augusta — never allowing Scheffler to pull clear despite the world's top-ranked player posting a bogey-free weekend. Scheffler's closing 68 was exemplary, but McIlroy's steady 71 was clinical and composed under intense pressure.

The 67, 65, 73, 71 scorecard tells the story: a man in control of his game, dictating rather than reacting. McIlroy made crucial saves when needed and struck telling blows when the moment demanded.

Scheffler's Bridesmaid Role

For Scheffler, the loss will sting, arriving as 5/1 favourite and the favourite's favourite. He posted a bogey-free final 36 holes (11 under par for two rounds) but it wasn't enough. McIlroy's margin of one reflects the fineness of major championship golf at the highest level. With 100 OWGR points and 750 FedEx Cup points awarded, Scheffler remains firmly entrenched at the summit of world golf but will be looking for redemption in May's PGA Championship at Aronimink.

The Chasing Pack

A tie for third place at 10 under embraced five players: Justin Rose, Cameron Young, Sam Burns, Tyrrell Hatton, and Russell Henley. Young's impressive form continues after his Players Championship victory, while Rose's consistent play in his comeback season earns credibility for his Augusta cause.

What's Next

The PGA Tour moves to South Carolina this week for the RBC Heritage at Harbour Town, before pivoting to the PGA Championship at Aronimink Golf Club, Philadelphia, in May. Meanwhile, in Mexico City this week, LIV Golf's high-altitude challenge at Club de Golf Chapultepec sets a markedly different tone. McIlroy's masterclass at Augusta will set the benchmark for consistency across both tours as spring golf hits its stride.
